name: Cloudflare GraphQL Introspection Blocking Baseline
summary: Detects and blocks GraphQL introspection query patterns (for example __schema and __type) on GraphQL endpoints.
description: This production-first baseline pack helps reduce GraphQL schema reconnaissance by detecting requests that include introspection fields such as __schema or __type when sent to GraphQL endpoints. Recommended rollout is log mode first to establish known-good behavior, then promote to block once validated.

phases: Deploy in log mode first (no block) for at least 7 days.
Review security events for legitimate developer tooling and approved internal scanners.
Add scoped exceptions for trusted sources if introspection is required in controlled environments.
Promote action to block in production once false positives are addressed.
Continuously monitor for bypass attempts and adjust endpoint/path matching as APIs evolve.

tuning_notes: Scope to known GraphQL paths (for example /graphql, /api/graphql) to reduce noise.
Consider allowing introspection only from trusted IP ranges, service tokens, or non-production environments.
Watch for encoded payload variants and multiline request bodies in POST operations.
If persisted queries are used, ensure rule logic still inspects request bodies where applicable.
Coordinate with API owners before blocking to avoid disrupting legitimate schema tooling.

kpis: Count of matched introspection requests by endpoint
Top source IPs / ASNs triggering matches
Block volume and downstream API error rates after enforcement
alerting: Spike in introspection matches over baseline
Repeated matches from single source over short window

rules.name: GraphQL introspection keyword on GraphQL endpoint
rules.description: Matches requests to GraphQL endpoints where the request body contains __schema or __type.
rules.notes: Start with log action, validate legitimate traffic, then change to block for production enforcement.

changes: Initial baseline pack for GraphQL introspection detection and staged blocking rollout.
